name: Benin (Republic of Benin / République du Bénin)
summary: Benin is a West African civil-law unitary republic whose family-law framework operates under the Persons and Family Code 2004 (Code des Personnes et de la Famille, Law 2002-07) drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage with substantial modernisation including gender equality codification, abolition of polygamy for civil marriages (replaced by monogamous marriage default), and codification of customary-law marriage provisions. Parental authority and child custody are governed by Persons and Family Code arts. 411-451. The Supreme Court (Cour Suprême) is the apex court for civil and criminal matters; the Constitutional Court (Cour Constitutionnelle) operates separate constitutional review. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Court of First Instance (Tribunal de Première Instance). Psychology profession is regulated through the Ministry of Health framework. Benin is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the interest-of-the-child standard. Benin acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 February 2018.

statutory_framework.title: Persons and Family Code — Parental authority and custody
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Persons and Family Code drawing on French civil-law substantive heritage with substantial modernisation including gender equality codification and monogamy default. Arts. 411-451 govern parental authority and child custody.

statutory_framework.title: Children's Code
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Children's Code aligned with UNCRC obligations.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court (Cour Suprême)
apex_courts.role: Apex court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Constitutional Court (Cour Constitutionnelle)
apex_courts.role: Constitutional Court with original jurisdiction over constitutional review.

professional_regulators.name: Ministry of Health, Benin
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of health and allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Beninese family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Dahomey independence + recurring-coup framework
key_developments.description: Dahomey (later Benin) independence 1 August 1960 from France — substantively distinctive West-African recurring-coup pattern 1963, 1965 (twice), 1967, 1969, 1972 (most-coup-prone African state in early independence period). Foundational pre-Kerekou-Marxist framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Mathieu Kerekou coup + People's Republic of Benin + Marxist-Leninist framework
key_developments.description: Mathieu Kerekou coup d'état 26 October 1972. Subsequent People's Republic of Benin (Dahomey renamed 1975) under Marxist-Leninist framework 1975-1990 — substantively distinctive West-African Marxist-Leninist framework. Substantive Cold-War-era political-institutional framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Benin national-conference + democratic-transition + multi-party-framework
key_developments.description: Benin National Conference of the Active Forces 19-28 February 1990 substantively distinctive globally African National-Conference framework (first of subsequent Francophone-African national-conferences). Substantive democratic-transition framework establishing Constitution of Benin 1990. Substantively significant African democratic-trajectory pattern.

key_developments.title: Constitution of Benin 1990 + multi-party-democratic framework
key_developments.description: Constitution of Benin 1990 adopted 11 December 1990 by referendum substantively reforming political-institutional framework establishing multi-party-democratic framework. Substantive constitutional anchor for contemporary family-law jurisprudence.

key_developments.title: Benin ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child
key_developments.description: Benin ratified the UNCRC on 3 August 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 2004 Persons and Family Code and 2015 Children's Code substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Persons and Family Code 2004
key_developments.description: Federal Persons and Family Code enacted Law 2002-07 of 24 August 2004 with substantial modernisation including gender equality codification and monogamy-default for civil marriages. Arts. 411-451 govern parental authority and child custody. Substantively distinctive West-African Francophone gender-equality-codification framework.

key_developments.title: Children's Code 2015 + UNCRC-aligned framework
key_developments.description: Federal Children's Code enacted Law 2015-08 of 8 December 2015 aligned with UNCRC obligations. Substantively significant child-protection-framework consolidation within West-African Francophone framework.

key_developments.title: Patrice Talon presidency + technocratic-reform framework + contested-democratic-trajectory
key_developments.description: Patrice Talon (independent businessman) elected President 6 March 2016 — substantively distinctive West-African technocratic-presidency framework. Subsequent 2019 electoral-law reform substantially affecting democratic-trajectory + 2021 contested re-election framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession + cross-border-framework
key_developments.description: Benin acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 February 2018 — substantively significant Hague West African accession affecting cross-border-jurisdiction-practice with France and other Hague-party states.

key_developments.title: Médiateur de la République + Sahel security framework + ECOWAS engagement
key_developments.description: Médiateur de la République (Ombudsperson) institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Persons and Family Code 2004 + Children's Code 2015 framework. Benin Sahel-spillover security framework substantively significant for northern-Benin family-law adjudication including IDP cases. ECOWAS engagement framework substantively significant West African institutional context. Substantively significant West African institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Cour Suprême + Cour Constitutionnelle — interest-of-the-child substantive register + Talon presidency
key_developments.description: Cour Suprême and Cour Constitutionnelle continue to develop interest-of-the-child jurisprudence under Persons and Family Code 2004 arts. 411-451 + Children's Code 2015 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within Patrice Talon presidency framework. Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Benin operates a French-civil-law family-law framework with gender equality codification and monogamy-default reform — places Benin in the West African Francophone civil-law cluster with French-derivative substantive heritage.
Persons and Family Code 2004 monogamy-default for civil marriages is structurally significant — among more progressive West African Francophone family-law reforms in the corpus.
Hague Convention 1980 accession 2018 places Benin in the Hague West African cluster.
editorial_notes: Benin jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 3 to 10 key_developments with full Dahomey-to-Talon trajectory: 1960-Dahomey-independence-+-recurring-coup + 1972-Mathieu-Kerekou-coup-+-People's-Republic-+-Marxist-Leninist + 1990-Benin-national-conference-+-democratic-transition + 1990-Constitution-of-Benin-+-multi-party-democratic + 1990-UNCRC-ratification + 2004-Persons-and-Family-Code-Law-2002-07 + 2015-Children's-Code-Law-2015-08 + 2016-Patrice-Talon-presidency-+-technocratic-reform + 2018-Hague-Convention-1980-accession + 2024-Cour-Suprême-+-Cour-Constitutionnelle-+-Talon-presidency.
Civil-law West African Francophone (Persons and Family Code 2004 + monogamy-default reform + Children's Code 2015 + Hague Convention 1980 accession 2018).

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive interest-of-the-child analysis under Persons and Family Code 2004 arts. 411-451 + Children's Code 2015 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins West-African + Francophone + civil-law + French-derivative + Persons-and-Family-Code-2004-gender-equality-+-monogamy-default-distinctive-West-African + Mathieu-Kerekou-Marxist-Leninist-People's-Republic-of-Benin-1975-1990 + Benin-National-Conference-1990-first-Francophone-African-distinctive + recurring-coup-1963-1972-most-coup-prone-African-state-early-independence + Patrice-Talon-technocratic-presidency-2016 + Hague-Convention-1980-accession-2018-West-African clusters within the corpus.
